title: Lathyrus subvillosus
taxonomy: Lathyrus subvillosus is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. This plant is classified as a perennial or annual legume. It is native to North America and is also known as hairy pea. Lathyrus subvillosus is a member of the genus Lathyrus, which includes other species of pea-like plants. The classification of Lathyrus subvillosus into its specific taxonomic ranks is as follows: kingdom: Plantae, clade: angiosperms, clade: eudicots, clade: rosids, order: Fabales, family: Fabaceae, genus: Lathyrus, species: L. subvillosus.
uses: Lathyrus subvillosus, also known as hairy vetchling, has been found to have potential medicinal uses. However, it's essential to note that more research is required to fully understand its effects. Some studies suggest that the plant may possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The plant's extracts have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Further investigation is necessary to confirm its efficacy and potential uses in traditional medicine.
benefits: Lathyrus subvillosus, also known as hairy vetchling, has been studied for its potential health benefits. The plant is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the body against free radicals and oxidative stress. Its antioxidant properties may also contribute to its anti-inflammatory effects, potentially relieving symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis. Additionally, hairy vetchling contains isoflavones, which may have a positive impact on cardiovascular health by reducing cholesterol levels and improving blood vessel function. Further research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of Lathyrus subvillosus.

preparations: Lathyrus subvillosus, also known as hairy vetchling, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. side_effects: Lathyrus subvillosus, also known as hairy pea, is a plant that should be consumed with caution. While it is edible, eating large quantities of its seeds can cause health issues. Consuming the seeds of Lathyrus subvillosus over an extended period may lead to neurolathyrism, a condition characterized by muscle weakness and paralysis. The condition is caused by a toxin called ODAP, which can be present in the seeds. It is essential to only consume the plant in moderation and in controlled amounts to minimize potential side effects.
intro: Lathyrus subvillosus, also known as hairy vetchling or hairy pea, is a perennial legume native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lathyrus subvillosus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Lathyrus subvillosus is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, often used to treat coughs, sore throats, and digestive issues.
